title: pinus quadrifolia subsp. juarezensis
intro: Pinus quadrifolia subsp. juarezensis is a subspecies of the Chihuahuan pine, a plant species native to Mexico, specifically in the state of Baja California Sur. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, and is also believed to have anti-cancer properties and to support immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being dried and used in traditional folk medicine.
